hook:
5 underrated tools making me $2,847/month passively.

Not affiliate links. Not sponsored.

Just stuff that actually works.
tweets:
Tool 1: Gumroad ($1,200/mo)

I sell a Notion template for $29.

Took 4 hours to make.
Sells 40-50 copies/month on autopilot.

No ads. Just SEO from one blog post I wrote in 2023.

The Gumroad trick nobody uses:

Add a "pay what you want" tier above your main price.

I have $29 (base) and $49 (support tier).

30% of buyers choose $49.

Free 30% revenue bump.

Tool 2: Lemon Squeezy ($640/mo)

Same concept but for a code snippet library.

$9 one-time purchase.

~70 sales/month.

Developers buy random stuff at 2 AM. Be there when they do.

Tool 3: Beehiiv referral payouts ($380/mo)

Beehiiv pays you when readers sign up for other newsletters.

I promote 3 newsletters I actually read.

$2-5 per signup. Adds up.

Tool 4: Carrd affiliate ($290/mo)

I recommend Carrd in tutorials.

30% recurring commission.

~$10/referral/year.

29 active referrals = $290/month forever.

Tool 5: RevenueCat (app subscriptions) ($337/mo)

One small app with 67 active subscribers at $4.99/month.

App took 2 weeks to build.

Runs itself now. Just server costs (~$5/mo).

The pattern:

1. Make something once
2. Put it where people search
3. Let it compound

No daily posting grind.
No client calls.
No trading time for money.

What doesn't work (stuff I tried):

- Amazon KDP (saturated, 3% royalties)
- Print on demand (returns killed margins)
- Stock photography (made $14 in 6 months)

Digital products with direct sales = the play.
cta:
Building more passive income streams this year.

Follow @PRINTMAXXER for the breakdown.

What's your best passive tool? Drop it below.
